steps.assistant_text: Vtable-entry dispatcher. The 99.75% NM body had a single residual: the address addu emitted product-rs (`addu rd,prod,base`) where the target emits base-rs (`addu rd,base,prod`), a coloring/coalesce tie that no plain C reorder could flip without breaking the $t9 coalesce. decomp-permuter (72k iters, -j6, ~500s) found two semantic-no-op nudges: `self[(0x48/4) ^ 0]` (identity XOR re-orders the index web to base-rs while keeping the $t9 coalesce) and `(new_var = 0x90)` (parks the +0x90 offset in a throwaway local, fixing the call-register temp number). Verified in-tree exact, not an isolated false-100.

metadata.verification: in-tree exact: 40/40 words, 0 non-reloc diffs, reloc records at 6 target offsets (D_arg_b1_1130 HI16/LO16, gl_func_00000000 x2 jal, D_cur_b1_1130 HI16/LO16); ROM byte-identical to baserom; cracked by decomp-permuter
